heading: Lactation room in public buildings
label: 3318 Lactation room in public buildings
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–30, § 2(c)133 Stat. 1033July 25, 2019“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall take effect 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”July 25, 2019
amendments: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–30, § 2(c)133 Stat. 1033July 25, 2019“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall take effect 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”July 25, 2019

children.heading: .—Appropriate authority
children.label: 1 .—Appropriate authority
children.text: The term “appropriate authority” means the head of a Federal agency, the Architect of the Capitol, or other official authority responsible for the operation of a public building.

children.heading: .—Covered public building
children.label: 2 .—Covered public building
children.text: The term “covered public building” means a public building (as defined in section 3301) that is open to the public and contains a public restroom, and includes a building listed in section 6301 or 5101.

children.label: C
children.text: contains a chair, a working surface, and, if the public building is otherwise supplied with electricity, an electrical outlet.

children.heading: .—Lactation Room Required
children.label: b .—Lactation Room Required
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c), the appropriate authority of a covered public building shall ensure that the building contains a lactation room that is made available for use by members of the public to express breast milk.

children.label: A
children.text: does not contain a lactation room for employees who work in the building; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not have a room that could be repurposed as a lactation room or a space that could be made private using portable materials, at a reasonable cost; or
new construction would be required to create a lactation room in the public building and the cost of such construction is unfeasible.

children.heading: .—No Unauthorized Entry
children.label: d .—No Unauthorized Entry
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize an individual to enter a public building or portion thereof that the individual is not otherwise authorized to enter.
